description:
You are a compliance officer auditing repeated login attempts that were logged by a security probe.  
    A raw text file already exists with one IPv4 address per line:

        /home/user/audit/raw_login_attempts.log

    Your assignment is to create a frequency report that shows how many times each address appears, sorted so that:
      1. Rows are ordered by descending occurrence count.
      2. Whenever two addresses have the same count, they are ordered by ordinary lexicographical (ASCII) order of the address string.

    Use only the standard GNU core-utils tools `sort` and `uniq` (and, if helpful, `head`, `tail`, `cat`, `cut`, etc.).  
    No Python, awk, perl, etc.—the idea is to demonstrate mastery of `sort | uniq -c | sort` style pipelines.

    Deliverables
    1. Create exactly one new CSV file at

        /home/user/audit/ip_frequency_report.csv

    2. The file **must** have this exact column header (all lowercase):

        count,ip

    3. Starting on the very next line, list every unique IP address that appears in the raw log together with its occurrence count, following the sort rules above.  
       Each data line must have the format

        <count>,<ip>

       where <count> is an integer with no leading zeroes and <ip> is the address string.

    4. The report must include **all** IPs present in the raw log and nothing else—no extra blank lines, no surrounding whitespace.

    Verification hints (do not print these, they are only to guide your work):
      • You can check the total number of lines in the report should be (unique-IP-count + 1 header).  
      • The raw file contains fewer than 20 lines, so a short command pipeline is sufficient.

    When you believe the task is complete you should be able to run:

        cat /home/user/audit/ip_frequency_report.csv

    and see a neatly sorted, header-first CSV matching the rules above.
truth:
<!--
        Initial system state that the automated tests will rely on.
    -->
    <precreated>
        <directory path="/home/user/audit" />
        <file path="/home/user/audit/raw_login_attempts.log"><![CDATA[
192.168.1.10
10.0.0.5
192.168.1.10
8.8.8.8
10.0.0.5
10.0.0.5
203.0.113.1
8.8.8.8
192.168.1.10
198.51.100.2
203.0.113.1
203.0.113.1
127.0.0.1
192.168.1.10
127.0.0.1
]]></file>
    </precreated>

    <!--
        Expected file that the agent must create.
    -->
    <expected>
        <file path="/home/user/audit/ip_frequency_report.csv"><![CDATA[
count,ip
4,192.168.1.10
3,10.0.0.5
3,203.0.113.1
2,127.0.0.1
2,8.8.8.8
1,198.51.100.2
]]></file>
    </expected>
